error_signature: n8n may have run out of memory while running this execution
title: N8N_DEFAULT_BINARY_DATA_MODE not taking effect in Docker
symptom: Despite setting N8N_DEFAULT_BINARY_DATA_MODE to filesystem, n8n continues to use RAM, leading to memory exhaustion errors.
not_this_if: Using a different execution mode or environment
root_cause: The environment variable N8N_DEFAULT_BINARY_DATA_MODE may not be correctly set in the Docker container, causing n8n to default to using RAM for binary data storage instead of the filesystem.
fix_steps: Ensure N8N_DEFAULT_BINARY_DATA_MODE is set to 'filesystem' in the docker-compose.yml file under the environment section.
Run 'docker compose down -v' to remove existing volumes.
Run 'docker compose up -d --build' to restart the container with the new settings.
Verify the environment variable is set correctly by running 'docker exec -it <container_name> env | grep BINARY'.
agent_instruction: Set N8N_DEFAULT_BINARY_DATA_MODE to 'filesystem' in docker-compose.yml and restart the container.
safety_notes: This process may lead to data loss if volumes are not backed up before removal.
